channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel refuses to withdraw from southern Lebanon despite US-Iran peace deal
item.description: Defense Minister Israel Katz declared Israel will keep forces in occupied areas of southern Lebanon "despite all current and future pressures," directly challenging a clause in the US-Iran agreement that called for de-escalation along the Lebanon-Israel border.

item.title: 🇮🇱⚖️ Israeli finance minister calls US-Iran deal "bad for Israel and the free world"
item.description: Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich criticized the US-Iran agreement brokered under Pakistani mediation, calling it detrimental to Israel and global security, in a sign of deep internal Israeli opposition to the deal.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇹🇷 Pakistan PM praises Erdogan's "highly supportive" role in US-Iran peace mediation
item.description: Pakistani Prime Minister Sharif publicly thanked Turkish President Erdogan for his crucial role in facilitating the US-Iran deal, also acknowledging contributions from Chinese, Saudi, and Qatari leaders to Islamabad's months-long mediation effort.

item.title: 🇪🇸🕊️ Spain's Sanchez hails US-Iran deal as end to "senselessness" and new era for Middle East
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez called the US-Iran agreement the end of "senselessness" and the start of a new era in the Middle East, urging the world to remember the costs of war and learn from the conflict.

item.title: 🇵🇸📉 West Bank economy teetering toward collapse under Israeli restrictions, report finds
item.description: A new report says Israel's web of restrictions is systematically dismantling the economy of the occupied West Bank, pushing it toward collapse and severely limiting economic opportunities for Palestinians.

item.title: 🇪🇺 ECB Chief Lagarde welcomes Iran ceasefire as good news, Nagel cautions on lasting inflation
item.description: ECB President Christine Lagarde hailed the US-Iran ceasefire deal as potentially reopening the Strait of Hormuz, but Bundesbank chief Joachim Nagel warned it would bring no immediate relief to elevated eurozone inflation.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel strikes Hezbollah targets in Beirut suburbs after projectiles hit northern Israel
item.description: Israel attacked Hezbollah targets in Beirut southern suburbs after the Iran-aligned group fired projectiles into Israeli territory. Lebanons state news agency reported two people killed in the strikes.

item.title: 🛢️ Trump announces Strait of Hormuz will reopen Friday under Iran peace deal
item.description: President Trump declared the Strait of Hormuz will reopen on Friday under the terms of the US-Iran peace agreement. Oil prices fell sharply on the announcement, with a formal signing ceremony set for Switzerland.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia bombards Kyiv, sets fire to Pechersk Lavra monastery, nine killed across Ukraine
item.description: A major Russian drone and missile attack set fire to Kyivs historic Pechersk Lavra monastery and killed at least nine people across Ukraine, including five rescuers in Kharkiv. It was the heaviest attack on Kyiv in two weeks.

item.title: 📈 Global shares and bonds surge after Iran peace deal eases inflation fears
item.description: Stock markets and bonds rallied worldwide while oil prices tumbled after the US-Iran peace deal promised to ease global inflationary pressures and reduce the need for higher interest rates. Asian markets led the gains.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Nine Killed, Historic Kyiv Monastery Ablaze After Massive Russian Strikes
item.description: Russia launched its heaviest air attack on Kyiv in two weeks, killing at least nine people and setting fire to the historic Pechersk Lavra monastery. Moscow denied striking the monastery, claiming a Ukrainian Patriot missile was responsible.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Refuses to Withdraw From Seized Land in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza
item.description: Israel's defense minister stated the country will not withdraw from territory captured in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza, directly contravening the interim US-Iran peace deal provisions. The statement signals potential friction with the agreement's implementation.

item.title: 🕊️ US and Iran Say Peace Agreement to Be Signed Friday in Geneva
item.description: The United States and Iran have announced a formal signing of their peace agreement for Friday in Switzerland, with preparatory talks planned in Doha. The deal promises an immediate and permanent end to military operations on all fronts.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Demands $12 Billion in Unfrozen Assets Before Formal Negotiations Begin
item.description: Tehran has set a precondition for advancing beyond the framework deal: the US must release approximately $12 billion in frozen Iranian assets before formal negotiations proceed, adding a significant hurdle to the peace process.

item.title: ⚔️ Indian Army Selects Israeli Meprolight MEPRO X6 as New Optic for Negev Machine Guns
item.description: The Indian Army has chosen Meprolight's MEPRO X6 compact 6x telescopic day sight, with a range of up to 1,000 meters, as the new daytime optic for its Negev light machine guns, deepening India-Israel defense ties.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel strikes southern Beirut, killing at least 3; issues forced displacement orders for 29 Lebanese towns
item.description: Israeli strikes on Beirut killed at least three people as the military issued forced displacement orders for 29 towns in southern Lebanon, raising concerns the escalation could derail the nascent US-Iran peace deal.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran and US agree to halt war and reopen Strait of Hormuz, sending oil prices tumbling
item.description: The preliminary pact between Washington and Tehran leaves the fate of Irans nuclear program to further negotiations. Brent crude futures fell 4% as markets priced in the reopening of the critical oil shipping chokepoint.

item.title: 🇿🇦 African migrants with deep roots in South Africa flee xenophobic attacks in Durban
item.description: Long-settled African migrants in Durban are fleeing xenophobic violence, with many having lived in South Africa since childhood. The displacement underscores rising anti-immigrant sentiment across the country.

item.title: 🏛️ G7 leaders convene in France after US and Iran declare agreement to end war
item.description: Leaders of the Group of Seven wealthy nations gather at Evian-les-Bains to discuss next steps on Iran, Ukraine, and global trade following the US-Iran peace deal announcement.

item.title: 🇫🇷 Trump heads to G7 summit after announcing US-Iran peace agreement
item.description: President Trump travels to the French Alps for the G7 summit fresh off declaring a peace deal with Iran, with Ukraine war and trade tensions also on the agenda.

item.title: ⚠️ Anti-G7 protesters torch Tesla and smash UN office windows in Geneva
item.description: Geneva police fired teargas at protesters who set fire to a Tesla vehicle and smashed windows at a UN agency during violent demonstrations ahead of the G7 summit across the border in France.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China AI stocks diverge as Zhipu soars while Minimax stumbles
item.description: A widening valuation gap between China's two top LLM startups signals investor分化tion in the Chinese AI sector, with Zhipu gaining while Minimax loses ground.

item.title: ✈️ European aviation fuel supply tightens as Iran war cuts imports to 80%
item.description: European aviation fuel imports remain at just 80% of pre-war levels despite US-Iran peace deal, with high prices and potential flight reductions expected in autumn.

item.title: 🇸🇬 Singapore retrenchments rise sharply among degree holders and older workers
item.description: Q1 2026 saw increased retrenchments in Singapore with the steepest increase among higher-educated workers, reflecting ongoing business restructuring in the city-state.

item.title: 🚢 Japan to resume domestic LNG carrier construction by 2035
item.description: Three major Japanese shipbuilders plan to restart domestic LNG carrier construction by 2035, seeking technology cooperation from Korean shipyards after years without production.
